recent_calls.summary: The user called after hours to report inaccurate technician notes and requested customer service follow-up. The agent documented the issue and informed the user that a representative would call back on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from the user.

The user called to confirm and request an appointment for cooling service, specifying a preference for either May 19th or 20th, ultimately confirming May 20th. The agent noted the request and informed the user that the team would follow up on the next business day. The user decided to call back tomorrow for further details.

The user, Rosie from First American Home Warranty, called Budget's after-hours line seeking a vendor to assist with service calls in their market. The agent informed Rosie that no live assistance was available after hours and took a message with Rosie's contact details. The call ended without a live conversation due to after-hours timing.

The user, Dianne, requested a cooling service appointment because her cooling system is not working and will not turn on. She scheduled the service for Wednesday, May 20th at 8 AM, and the agent confirmed the service address and details. The agent informed her that the team will confirm availability on the next business day.

Yolanda Valentine Brown Ridley Brown called to make a service appointment for a non-working heating and cooling thermostat under her home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details including the service address, phone number, and home warranty dispatch number, and confirmed a Thursday morning appointment preference. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the call ended successfully with a promise of follow-up from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing.
The user inquired about the status of a technician visit, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Danica Williams called to clarify that she had already completed the intake form and is now waiting for scheduling related to a service request under a home warranty with American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the nature of the service request and asked for details about the type of service needed.

The user called to request the part number for a compressor related to a cooling system repair at their home. The agent was unable to provide the part number directly but offered to connect the user with a licensed technician. The call ended with the user being transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The agent greeted the user and asked for the service address ZIP code, but the user hung up shortly after without responding.

The user called to confirm that they had operated their system correctly and did not want to turn it off unnecessarily. The agent acknowledged the concern and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Daniel called to report that his air conditioning unit is completely out and the outside line is frozen. He requested service as soon as possible and booked an appointment for the next day at 4 PM. The agent confirmed the details and informed him about the dispatch fee, and the call ended successfully.

Tamara Thurman called to schedule a service appointment for her older cooling unit that needs to be recharged under her American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed details, collected the dispatch number, and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her soon. Tamara provided positive feedback and declined additional information about maintenance programs at this time.
The user called to return a phone call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
Cynthia Tate returned a call regarding a free air conditioning check appointment and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer.
The call was very brief with the agent attempting to engage the user, but the user did not respond meaningfully and ended the call shortly after. No service or information requests were made.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent upon request. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without further interaction.
The user called but quickly realized they had the wrong number and ended the call. The agent politely acknowledged this and offered future assistance if needed.

Vince from American Home Shield called to confirm that a new work order for air conditioning repair, not a checkup, was required and to cancel the previously discussed service order. The agent confirmed details including the service address and dispatch number, and agreed to pass along the cancellation request. The call ended with no further requests from Vince.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent's initial greeting without any user response or interaction.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another representative. The call lasted 19 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user accidentally disconnected the call early and then reconnected. The agent reassured the user that nothing important was missed, and the user confirmed that everything was fine. The call ended shortly after without further discussion.

Talise called to reschedule a heating and cooling service appointment due to an emergency and requested to be contacted in about a week or on a weekend. The agent confirmed the service details, including issues with the air conditioning and HVAC noise, and noted Talise's interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs. The call ended with the agent confirming that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will reach out soon.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called and requested to speak with a live representative. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent to assist the user further.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for their cooling system through their home warranty but had trouble submitting the request online. The agent confirmed the details and attempted to schedule the appointment for the next day, then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance with the dispatch number. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

Renata Taylor called to inquire about scheduling a home warranty claim for her air conditioning, which stopped cooling after running briefly. The agent confirmed details including the dispatch number, service address, and service type, and informed her that the team would contact her to schedule the appointment. Renata also expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs.

The user called to check the status of a claim and provided the dispatch ID number to the agent. The agent confirmed the information and assured the user that a team member would follow up soon. The call ended with the user expressing thanks and no further requests.
The user requested to cancel today's appointment and then asked to speak with a live agent. The call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the technician's arrival time. The agent confirmed the user's details and found the appointment scheduled for 11 AM to 12 PM, but since it was already past that time, the call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to check on their technician. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

The user called to discuss charges and specifically requested to speak with Angie. The agent apologized for not having direct billing access and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user, Jackie Qualls, reported an air conditioning unit not cooling and requested a service appointment for Wednesday morning, the twentieth. The agent collected all necessary information, confirmed the details, and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to inquire about the cost of checking their air conditioning system. The initial agent was unable to provide pricing details and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the schedule for an air conditioning check at their service address. The agent collected the ZIP code and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 19 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called Budget and requested customer service. The agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent to assist the user further.

The user, Sheila, called to inquire about repeated emails indicating that a signed document was not received despite her signing it. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Michael called to inquire about the status of his HVAC technician appointment. The agent was unable to find an appointment in the system and offered to transfer him to a live representative, which Michael accepted. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to report an issue with a DocuSign document not being recognized as signed despite signing it. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Janet called to request a technician to check her air conditioner, which was installed last July and is running but not blowing much air. She preferred an afternoon appointment on Wednesday, and the agent successfully booked her for 2 PM on May 20, 2026. The call ended with confirmation of the booking and a polite closing.
The user requested to speak with a supervisor, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.

The user reported that their air conditioning unit installed by Budget is not cooling and requested a service appointment. The agent booked an appointment for the next day at noon and confirmed the user's contact details. The user was satisfied with the interaction and gave positive feedback.
Kimberly from American Home Shield called to discuss a dispatch and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 24 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user asked to speak with Amy, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate live agent successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user, Scott, called to schedule an air conditioning checkup at his Edwardsville address for June 10th in the afternoon. The agent confirmed the details but had no available slots for that date and offered to contact him when availability opens. The call ended with the agent transferring Scott to a live representative for further assistance.

David Kitt called to request HVAC service for a four ton central air unit with a wiring issue located on top of a food trailer at 1600 Mid Rivers Mall, Saint Peters, Missouri. The agent confirmed the details and informed David that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him shortly. David declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Alicia from American Home Shield called to follow up on a dispatch and inquire about out-of-pocket costs. The agent, Sophie, informed her that billing information was not accessible and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
